title: On the Architecture of Entropy
author: From: The Second Law of Thermodynamics
content: Systems naturally trend toward disorder. Without the constant application of energy (effort/maintenance), your projects will naturally decay.
action: Action: Perform a 'maintenance' audit—what part of your workflow is currently sliding into disorder?

title: The Theory of Constraints
author: From: Eliyahu Goldratt
content: Every complex system has one bottleneck. Improving anything other than the bottleneck is an illusion of progress.
action: Action: Find the single biggest delay in your current project and apply all your focus there.

title: The Availability Bias
author: From: Psychology
content: We judge importance by how recently we heard about something. Don't let the 'loudest' task be the 'most important' task.
action: Action: Filter your to-do list: ignore all recent requests and focus only on the mission-critical items.

title: The Hindsight Bias
author: From: Daniel Kahneman
content: We believe past events were more predictable than they actually were. This gives us false confidence in our ability to predict the future.
action: Action: Acknowledge that your next project's outcome is inherently uncertain and build a 'plan B'.

title: The Law of Diffusion of Innovation
author: From: Everett Rogers
content: Change happens in waves. Don't be frustrated if others don't 'get' your idea yet; wait for the system to catch up.
action: Action: If you are hitting resistance on a new project idea, identify one 'early adopter' to test it with instead of trying to convince everyone.
